_comment: MOCK of SOURCE 1 for the COUPON subsystem: the PUBLIC on-chain coupon state, shaped to map 1:1 onto a future Coupon / CouponFactory contract (no contract exists yet — this subsystem is frontend-only for now). The frontend would read these via ethers.js: the coupon list = CouponFactory.getCoupons(); per coupon the getters (couponAddress, owner, value, redeemed, redeemedAt, claimTokenHash, createdAt). `value` is the chain's NATIVE coin and is shown as a human-readable whole-coin number (on-chain it is wei, converted via parseEther/formatEther at the ethers boundary) — same convention as contractData.json. `couponAddress` is the coupon keypair's ADDRESS (the 'public key'); redemption verifies a signature against it via ecrecover, so the PRIVATE key is never on-chain. Joined to the off-chain secret store (couponSecrets.mock.ts) by `id`. Anything the UI shows that looks computed is DERIVED in couponsService: status (pending/active/redeemed) from owner+redeemed; the EUR nominal from value x the placeholder rate; the explorer URL + short address from couponAddress; the 'Erstellt' date from createdAt. owner = 0x000...000 means unclaimed (Ausstehend). The addresses below are REAL generated keypair addresses whose private keys live in couponSecrets.mock.ts, so the claim->reveal->redeem demo actually verifies end to end. The pre-claimed coupons (#1-#4) are deliberately owned by wallets that are NOT selectable login personas, so the ownership gate is honest: the claim demo runs through the PENDING coupon #5 (token DEMO-CLAIM-TOKEN-5) — the first wallet to open that link becomes its owner — while opening an already-owned coupon's link shows the 'belongs to another wallet' rejection.
